```{r}
# Chemical Liability Scoring Framework (Max-Based Tier System)
#
# This scoring system assigns compounds to liability tiers based on the
# highest-severity flag present rather than summing penalties. This avoids
# artificial inflation when multiple related alerts are triggered.
# The PAINS/MLSMR/BMS/GSK/Glaxo/LINT/Dundee/Inpharmatica/SureChEMBL are based on the the ChEMBL database and Pat Walters work.
# See https://github.com/PatWalters/rd_filters.git

# Tier definitions:

#   Score 10 (ELEVATED):
#     - PAINS compounds 
#     - Cytotoxicity (≥25% viability loss)
#
#   Score 5 (MODERATE):
#     - Industry structural alerts (MLSMR / NIH structural alerts, BMS, GSK, Glaxo, LINT, Dundee)
#     - tdTomato reporter suppression (≥25% loss)
#
#   Score 2 (MINOR):
#     - Lipinski rule-of-five violations
#     - Inpharmatica / SureChEMBL alerts

# Risk categories:
#   10  -> "ELEVATED"
#   5   -> "MODERATE"
#   2   -> "MINOR"
#   0   -> "CLEAN"

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

df_final <- df_hits %>%
  left_join(properties_tbl, by = "SMILES_used", suffix = c(".old", "")) %>%
  select(-ends_with(".old")) %>%
  mutate(
    Viability = if ("Viability" %in% names(.)) Viability else NA_real_,
    TdTomato  = if ("tdTomato_Diff" %in% names(.)) tdTomato_Diff <= -25 else FALSE,
    Cytotoxic = !is.na(Viability) & (Viability <= -25),

    has_smiles = !is.na(SMILES_used) & nzchar(trimws(SMILES_used)),
    flags = if_else(has_smiles, coalesce(alert_flags, ""), ""),

    # --- Flag categories ---
    High_Flag = grepl("PAINS", flags, ignore.case = TRUE) | Cytotoxic,

    Moderate_Flag = grepl("BMS|GLAXO|GSK|LINT|DUNDEE|MLSMR|NIH", flags, ignore.case = TRUE) | TdTomato,

    Mild_Flag = grepl("INPHARMATICA|SURECHEMBL", flags, ignore.case = TRUE) |
                (!is.na(Lipinski_Failed) & Lipinski_Failed > 0),

    # --- Max-based score ---
    AlertScore = pmax(
      if_else(High_Flag, 10L, 0L),
      if_else(Moderate_Flag, 5L, 0L),
      if_else(Mild_Flag, 2L, 0L)
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Risk = case_when(
      !has_smiles        ~ "UNKNOWN",
      AlertScore >= 10   ~ "ELEVATED",
      AlertScore >= 5    ~ "MODERATE",
      AlertScore >= 2    ~ "MINOR",
      TRUE               ~ "CLEAN"
    )
  )
 
 
chem_cols_chr <- c("flags", "Lipinski", "Lipinski_Failed")
chem_cols_num <- c("AlertScore", "MW", "LogP", "HBD", "HBA")
chem_cols_lgl <- c("PAINS_NIH_Flagged", "Industry_Flagged", "Other_Flagged")

df_final <- df_final %>%
  mutate(
    across(any_of(chem_cols_num), ~ if_else(has_smiles, .x, NA_real_)),
    across(any_of(chem_cols_chr), ~ if_else(has_smiles, .x, NA_character_)),
    across(any_of(chem_cols_lgl), ~ if_else(has_smiles, .x, NA))
  ) %>%
  select(-has_smiles)


#Select important columns for exporting:
df_final <- df_final %>%
  select(
    Plate, Well, BZScore, Viability, PlateZPrimeScore,
    PercentEffect, AvgFoci, CellCountPercentDiff, CellCount, ZScore,
    BScore, AssayType, Average_foci_perCell, Normalized_Effect, Reference,
    CAS,
    Systematic_error, QC_alpha,
    Risk, Lipinski, TdTomato, Cytotoxic,
    MW, LogP, HBD, HBA,
    Lipinski_Failed,
    SMILES_used, AlertScore, alert_flags
  )

# ============================================================
# 8) Write back to Excel
# ============================================================

#Overwrite previous hits sheet in original workbook to add chemical triage labels:

sheet_hits <- "Hits"

# (optional) make factors safe for Excel
df_final <- df_final %>% mutate(across(where(is.factor), as.character))

# 2) Load the existing workbook and overwrite the Hits sheet
wb <- loadWorkbook(fileX)


if (sheet_hits %in% names(wb)) removeWorksheet(wb, sheet_hits)
addWorksheet(wb, sheet_hits)

writeData(wb, sheet_hits, df_final, startCol = 1, startRow = 1, colNames = TRUE)
freezePane(wb, sheet_hits, firstActiveRow = 2)
addFilter(wb, sheet_hits, rows = 1, cols = 1:ncol(df_final))

# Desired sheets (in order)
new_order <- c("Hits", "Combined Analysis", "Plate Statistics", "Controls")

# Remove unwanted sheets
for (sh in setdiff(names(wb), new_order)) {
  removeWorksheet(wb, sh)
}

# Reorder by matching positions
worksheetOrder(wb) <- match(new_order, names(wb))

saveWorkbook(wb, fileX, overwrite = TRUE)

```
